{{Quote|Aliens have shed alien blood! But for what? Did you gain peace? Freedom from tyranny? No! The search for revenge has only brought you death and given you cause to distrust each other. Isn't this exactly what the Diversity Alliance promised to prevent?|Kur speaking to Diversity Alliance members during the [[Battle of Ryloth (Diversity Alliance Crisis)|fighting on Ryloth]]|<ref name="The Emperor's Plague">''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Emperor's Plague]]''</ref>}}
'''Kur''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who served as head of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth's]] government in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]].<ref name="The Emperor's Plague" /> He had formerly been a clan leader until his clan was defeated by [[Nolaa Tarkona]], founder of the anti-[[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Diversity Alliance]]. Instead of being banished to the [[Bright Lands]] as many other previous clan leaders were, Kur was banished to the cold wastes of Ryloth's night side. In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], he was rescued by [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]]—also on the run from Nolaa Tarkona.<ref name="Jedi Bounty">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Bounty]]''</ref> Later, he accompanied a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] inspection team to [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] to investigate reports of the Diversity Alliance keeping Human [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] [[Miner/Legends|miners]] and stocking up weapons to wage war on the New Republic. The inspection quickly escalated into a [[Battle of Ryloth (Diversity Alliance Crisis)|firefight]], yet Kur stepped forward and convinced everyone to lay down their arms. Following the mission, the people of Ryloth appointed Kur head of government. As one of his first acts in office, Kur sent two delegates to the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]], one male Twi'lek and one female Twi'lek.<ref name="The Emperor's Plague" />
